title: AbbVie Highlights Dermatology Data at 2026 AAD Annual Meeting
text: AbbVie announced new clinical and real-world evidence across its dermatology portfolio for presentation at the 2026 American Academy of Dermatology Annual Meeting. The data included evidence for risankizumab in psoriatic disease and upadacitinib in atopic dermatitis, vitiligo, and alopecia areata. AbbVie said it would present 24 abstracts, including late-breaking data, highlighting durable treatment responses, long-term safety, quality-of-life improvement, and disease control in immune-mediated skin diseases. This supports AbbVie's broader immunology strategy by reinforcing the role of Skyrizi and Rinvoq as important growth products. The news is relevant evidence for RAG explanations because it connects AbbVie's recent growth to pipeline expansion, additional indications, and clinical data that may help offset Humira's declining sales.
